data.name: Ohio Attorney General'S Office
data.description: Acts as attorney for the State of Ohio and its departments, boards, and agencies and is responsible for the collection of debts owed to the state. The Attorney General's Office has enforcement authority in areas empowered by the General Assembly. These areas include: consumer protection, charitable solicitation, antitrust actions, and organized crime. The office works with local law enforcement agencies at their request and occasionally, at the invitation of local prosecutors, the Office will serve as special prosecutors in criminal cases.

services.name: Esorn - Electronic Sex Offender Registration And Notification
services.description: Maintains a searchable database with information about sexual offenders in Ohio. Information offered includes the offender's name, current address, convicted offenses, and picture.  Offers notification by E-mail whenever a registered sex offender moves to a location within one mile of a specified address in Ohio.

schedules.description: 24 hrs a day, 7 days a week.

services.applicationProcess: Go to the Web site anytime to access information or to sign-up for E-mail notification. May phone for information M-F: 8:00am-8:00pm (questions about the program - will not conduct a search).

eligibility.description: No restrictions; E-mail notification only for addresses in Ohio.

services.name: Patriot Program (PRO BONO ATTORNEY GENERAL'S ASSISTANCE)
services.description: Provides volunteer attorneys that offer legal assistance to personnel of the armed forces who are enlisted in the reserves or National Guard and are currently activated, or are on active duty.  Assists enlisted personnel and their families in exercising their rights under the Soldiers' and Sailors Civil Relief Act.  Also, will provide legal assistance for wills, powers of attorney, living wills, and durable power of attorney for health care. Will not assist with legal matters such as divorce and child custody (does not represent in court).
services.applicationProcess: Phone M-F: 8:00am-7:00pm.  Will need to give full name, branch of service, full address, phone number, and type of legal assistance needed. Client will be contacted by an attorney or administrator in their area (most will be connected with someone in the Columbus, Cleveland, Cincinnati, or Toledo area).

eligibility.description: Residents of the State of Ohio who are active duty military personnel or who are enlisted in the reserves or National Guard and are currently activated. Assists families of enlisted personnel.

documents.description: Will be advised of all needed documents depending on the case; will need to show proof of military status.

services.name: Webcheck / Web Check
services.description: Provides a criminal background check to determine if an individual has a criminal record. Fingerprints are taken and compared against a database of criminal fingerprints. Two types of searches are offered: a Bureau of Criminal Identification and Investigation (BCI) check and a Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) check. The BCI check searches for offenses in Ohio, while the FBI check searches for offenses nationwide. Many state laws require background checks for certain occupations such as those who work with children or older adults, certain licensed professionals, and those in the financial industry.

schedules.description: Information line is staffed M-F: 8:00am-4:30pm. Hours for businesses where submission takes place varies by site.

services.applicationProcess: See web site anytime (Click on "Services" and then "background check") for a list of businesses offering the service and their contact information, and then contact the businesses directly.  May phone for general information M-F: 8:00am-4:30pm.

fees.description: Fixed fee (amount will vary depending on which business is submitting the search). No refund or credit will be awarded to those who cannot complete a search due to errors on the submission.

eligibility.description: Serves Ohio. BCI check can be conducted on anyone requesting the check. The Bureau of Criminal Investigations can only conduct an FBI check in cases where Ohio law requires the check and cannot be used for immigration purposes.

documents.description: Must have a valid photo I.D.: Driver's license or State I.D.

services.name: Complaints Concerning Ohio'S Lemon Law
services.description: Enforces and takes complaints concerning Ohio's Lemon Law. The Lemon Law requires auto manufacturers to repair or replace a new motor vehicle that has a problem or problems, covered by the warranty, that substantially impair the use, value or safety of the vehicle. The lemon law covers problems that occur within the first year of purchase or the first 18,000 miles of the vehicle, whichever comes first. The law requires manufacturers to notify buyers of their right to compensation if a vehicle is defective and establishes a consumer’s right to be refunded if the vehicle is not repaired or replaced within a reasonable period. Consumers may also look for information about complaints a company may have recieved through the Attorney General's Office as well as information as to how the dispute was resolved. May also search for lawsuits the office has filed against individuals and businesses that violate the state’s consumer laws.
services.applicationProcess: For instructions about submitting a complaint phone M-F: 8:00am-7:00pm or go to Website anytime. Complaints are submitted by phone, mail or through the Web site. Visit website to search for lawsuits the office has filed against individuals and businesses that violate the state’s consumer laws.

eligibility.description: Serves residents of Ohio who have a consumer complaint concerning a new car that is within the first year of purchase or the first 18,000 miles of the vehicle, whichever comes first.

documents.description: Copies of contracts, sales slips, advertisements, warranties, pertinent letters should be included with complaint.

services.name: Ohio Crime Victims Compensation Program
services.description: Administers the Ohio Crime Victims Compensation Program. Assists innocent victims of violent crime (resulting in injury) in recovering their economic losses suffered as a result of the crime. Maximum award of benefits to each victim per incident is $50,000. Assists only when recipient's funds are exhausted and there is no other source of compensation (such as insurance or settlement payments). Expenses eligible for compensation include reasonable medical expenses, mental health counseling (for specific types of crimes), ambulance services, funeral expenses, loss of wages, eyeglasses, dental work, replacement services (services victim can no longer perform), crime scene clean-up, evidence replacement, attorney fees relating to the application process, and other necessary services.
services.applicationProcess: Visit website to download an application or to register and file a claim online. May also phone for information during service hours. Minors must have a parent, guardian, or person responsible for expenses file the claim. Crimes must be reported to the law within 72 hrs. after the incident, or show just cause for delay. Claim must be filed within 2 years from the date the crime occurred (except minor victims have until their 20th birthday to file a claim). If the victim lives in Ohio but the crime occurred outside Ohio, it is preferred that the victim file first in the state where the crime occurred.

eligibility.description: Innocent victims of a violent crime (resulting in injury) that occured in Ohio, also includes dependents of a deceased victim, parents or guardians of a crime victim if they paid the victim's expenses or someone who has taken legal responsibility to pay victim's expenses. Must have exhausted or have no other collateral source of payment. Other eligibility requirements may apply, inquire. THOSE NOT ELIGIBLE: Engaged in illegal activity at time of crime; Convicted of a felony within 10 years prior to crime; Convicted of a felony while claim is pending; Incarcerated persons; Offenders or accomplice of the offender; Those whose own behavior is determined to have contributed to the crime (may still possibly receive reduced benefits).

services.name: Consumer Complaints/Information
services.description: Enforces the Ohio Consumer Sales Practices Act and the Ohio Homebuyers’ Protection Act. Investigates complaints related to consumer protection laws and mediates disputes when warranted.  Consumers may also look for information about complaints a company may have recieved through the Attorney General's Office as well as information as to how the dispute was resolved. Areas of inquiry include retail purchases, for-profit tax preparers, Internet auctions, pyramid schemes, advertising, business opportunities, home improvements, consumer credit, health spas, mail order businesses, new car parts, automobile repairs repairs, tax preparation companies, etc.  Will investigate (and if warranted, prosecute) for-profit mortgage foreclosure counseling companies. Can take complaints on mortgage servicers concerning escrow issues and problems dealing with the application of payments. Cannot address complaints against mortage services concerning modifications or foreclosure issues. Cannot take complaints against banks. Does not provide legal advice or representation as a private attorney. Cannot handle consumer issues regarding the purchase of used cars, public utilities, doctors, lawyers, insurance, real estate, financial investments, and state agencies. Offers consumer tips, printed information, and presentations about consumer issues. May also search for lawsuits the office has filed against individuals and businesses that violate the state’s consumer laws.

schedules.description: M-F 8:00am-7:00pm, Website: 24 hrs a day, 7 days a week.

services.applicationProcess: For instructions about submitting a complaint phone M-F: 8:00am-7:00pm or go to Web site anytime. Complaints are submitted by mail or through the Web site. Visit website to search for lawsuits the office has filed against individuals and businesses that violate the state’s consumer laws.

eligibility.description: No restrictions.

documents.description: Copies of contracts, sales slips, advertisements, warranties, pertinent letters should be included with complaint.

services.name: Indigent Burial - Information Only
services.description: The Ohio Revised Code contains rules concerning burial or cremation of deceased indigent persons (those with no savings that could pay for the burial or cremation and with no next of kin to provide these services) at the expense of townships or other municipal corporations such as villages and cities. In lieu of burying or cremating the unclaimed body of a deceased person, a board of township trustees may donate the body to a college (for research and/or dissection) or to the Board of Embalmers and Funeral Directors (for the purpose of education on embalming).

schedules.description: Generally, free to qualifying individuals or households.

services.applicationProcess: Caller should contact the local government (township, village, or city - city hall would be a good place to start) in which the deceased once lived for more information.

eligibility.description: These guidelines may be handled differently from one municipality to another and depending on the situation. In general, there is no guarantee of service.

services.name: Identity Theft Victim'S Assistance
services.description: Provides victims of identity theft a means of demonstrating to creditors that their identity has been stolen. Will assist in efforts to show creditors that fraudulent charges have been made against accounts in their name. Victims are given step-by-step information about how to alert creditors to fraudulent activity in their names, and simple, fill-in-the-blank affidavits to send to credit bureaus and creditors. Also offers general information about steps to take if one believes their identity has been stolen, as well as information about how to avoid identity theft.
services.applicationProcess: Vist website for more information and for the Identity Theft Notification and Affidavit form - to locate on website, scroll down and click on "Consumers" under Individuals & Families - on next page on the left hand side of the screen click on "Identity Theft. May also phone for assistance.

eligibility.description: Ohio residents who are victims of an identity theft (retroactive to incidences or cases reported since 1997). However, information is available to anyone.

documents.description: Completed Identity Theft Notification and Affidavit form.

services.name: 911 Good Samaritan Law
services.description: Provides qualified individuals with immunity from arrest, filing of charges, prosecution, conviction, and imposition of penalties for minor drug possession offenses when the evidence of the offense was obtained as a result of seeking or obtaining medical assistance for their own or another person's drug overdose or as a result of another person's seeking medical assistance for the client's drug overdose. "Seeking or obtaining medical assistance" includes, but is not limited to, making a 911 call, contacting in person or by telephone call an on-duty peace officer, or transporting or presenting a person to a health care facility. Within 30 days of the incident, the client must seek and obtain a screening and receive a referral for treatment from a community addiction services provider or a properly credentialed addiction treatment professional. If it is later determined that the individual does not qualify for immunity or has not taken the steps required to receive immunity, or if subsequent testing reveals that the evidence supports an offense that is more serious than a minor drug possession offense, the prosecutor will still have the ability to prosecute the individual.

schedules.description: 24 hours a day, 7 days a week.

services.applicationProcess: Attending peace officer will provide written information to the individual that they qualify for immunity under the law and the steps that they will have to take to receive immunity (or in some cases, the client may receive a letter in the mail informing them that they may be eligible for immunity). Officer will note in their report that this written information was provided to the individual. This office does not provide legal advice or assistance, clients are encouraged to seek help from their local Public Defender's Office or seek the help of an attorney, if needed.

eligibility.description: Applies to minor drug possession offenses only (a misdemeanor or fifth degree felony). The evidence upon which the minor drug possession charge would be based must have been obtained as a result of seeking medical assistance for their own or another person's drug overdose or as a result of another person's seeking medical assistance for the client's drug overdose. Must not be on Community Control (parole or probation) and may only receive immunity twice.

documents.description: Upon the request of any prosecuting attorney, the "qualified individual" must submit documentation to the prosecuting attorney that verifies that they, within 30 days, sought and obtained a screening and received a referral for treatment from a community addiction services provider or a properly credentialed addiction treatment professional.

services.name: Attorney General - State Of Ohio
services.description: Acts as attorney for the State of Ohio and its departments, boards, and agencies and is responsible for the collection of debts owed to the state. The Attorney General's Office has enforcement authority in areas empowered by the General Assembly. These areas include: consumer protection, charitable solicitation, antitrust actions, and organized crime. The office works with local law enforcement agencies at their request and occasionally, at the invitation of local prosecutors, the Office will serve as special prosecutors in criminal cases.
services.applicationProcess: Phone for information M-F: 8:00am-5:30pm or see Web site anytime.

eligibility.description: No restrictions.

services.name: Robocall Enforcement Unit / Ohio Protects
services.description: Accepts complaints of malicious robocalls. Note: this may not stop the calls from occurring but will assist the State in investigating suspected violators, taking enforcement action and seeking legislation to expand the state’s authority over robocalls.
services.applicationProcess: Visit website anytime for more information or to log a complaint. May also text or phone and the caller will be asked to answer a set of questions.

eligibility.description: Those who live in Ohio receiving robocalls.

services.name: Complaints/Information About Charities
services.description: Regulates and provides information on groups that may solicit funds for charitable purposes in the State of Ohio. Keeps records of charitable organizations that are licensed with the State of Ohio and may provide financial statements of these organizations. Handles complaints about charities and provides information about which groups may raise funds for other agencies. Licenses charitable bingo games and offers information about them.
services.applicationProcess: Phone for information or to register a complaint M-F: 8:00am-7:00pm. Information, forms for licensing and complaints available on Web site anytime.

fees.description: Fixed fee (minimal) for copies of records; Fixed fee for Bingo licenses and filing fee for agencies.

eligibility.description: No restrictions.

services.name: Health Care Fraud
services.description: Handles complaints and investigates issues dealing with suspected Medicaid fraud by providers and possible neglect or abuse of residents of long term care facilities in Ohio. Will prosecute if criminal action is found. Complaints addressed include, but not limited to, suspected Medicaid fraud by providers (billing for services not performed, over billing, kickbacks etc.) and possible neglect or abuse of residents of a long term care facility (such as nursing homes, group homes, MR/DD facilities, and hospital long term care units).  Does not represent the person making the complaint as a private attorney would.
services.applicationProcess: Phone for information or to make a complaint M-F: 8:00am-7:00pm.

eligibility.description: No restrictions.

services.name: Vine (VICTIM INFORMATION AND NOTIFICATION EVERYDAY)
services.description: Provides a free, anonymous, computer based service that immediately notifies registered users upon a change in an offender's status (release, transfer etc.). Computer generated notification calls will be made anytime there is a change in status and continues every half-hour for 24 hours (or leaves a message on answering machine every two hours) until client enters an identification number. In some cases, a notification letter will be sent. Has information about state prisons, some county jails, and some juvenile facilities. Does not maintain municipal information. Will also provide just the location and status of an offender.

schedules.description: 24 hrs a day, 7 days a week.

services.applicationProcess: Phone for information 24 hrs a day, 7 days a week. Using a touch-tone phone, caller registers by entering his/her phone number and an identification number. When there is a change in offender's status, the caller will be contacted with a computer generated message or by mail in some cases. There is an option to speak with someone. May also use website anytime.

eligibility.description: No restrictions. Targeted to those who have been crime victims, but not limited to them.

documents.description: Need offender's name or inmate number.

services.name: Crime Reporting
services.description: Provides an means for those who wish to report criminal activity, but who feel they have not received a reasonable response from their local law enforcement or who feel that reporting the crime to local authorities might compromise their personal security. Accepts reports of and investigates suspected human trafficking, drug activity, organized animal fighting, environmental/pollution concerns, public corruption, and child molestation in Ohio. Calls will remain confidential upon request.
services.applicationProcess: Phone for service M-F: 8:00am-7:00pm or use Web site anytime. Calls go to the general help-line where a representative will take the information and then forward it to the proper channels.

eligibility.description: No restrictions.

services.name: Ohio Missing Children Clearinghouse / Ohio Amber Plan
services.description: Acts as a missing persons clearinghouse for the State of Ohio. Provides information on and assists in the search of missing persons.  Missing persons generally include runaways, abductions (by relatives and non-relatives), lost children, persons with mental health issues or disabilities, and older adults.  Accepts reports of leads and sightings of missing persons.  Posts photos of missing persons on the Internet. Offers printed materials, presentations, a school curriculum, and fingerprint ID cards. Works with other government offices and the media to develop and maintain a plan for issuing Amber Alerts, which provide information to the public about abducted children and missing adults with health issues. Amber Alerts are only sent when a strict set of criteria is met.

schedules.description: 24 hrs a day, 7 days a week.

services.applicationProcess: Before assistance can be given to parents of missing children, a report must first be filed with law enforcement officials.  Phone for information or assistance 24 hrs a day, 7 days a week. Phone for information on current Amber alerts 24 hrs a day, 7 days a week.

eligibility.description: No restrictions for information or to provide information about missing persons. To have photos distributed, must be a legal guardian of a missing child or have power of attorney over missing adult. Fingerprint ID cards mainly for groups. Ohio Amber Plan serves Ohio.

locations.name: Ohio Attorney General'S Office
locations.description: Acts as attorney for the State of Ohio and its departments, boards, and agencies and is responsible for the collection of debts owed to the state. The Attorney General's Office has enforcement authority in areas empowered by the General Assembly. These areas include: consumer protection, charitable solicitation, antitrust actions, and organized crime. The office works with local law enforcement agencies at their request and occasionally, at the invitation of local prosecutors, the Office will serve as special prosecutors in criminal cases.

locations.name: Ohio Attorney General'S Office - Toledo Office
locations.description: Functions as the Regional Office of the Ohio Attorney General.

schedules.description: M-F: 8:00am-5:30pm, staff not always in the office.

addresses.street: 640 Jackson St., Suite 1340 One Government Center

locations.name: Ohio Attorney General'S Office - Crime Victims Services
locations.description: Reviews applications for compensation filed under Ohio's Crime Victims Compensation Law, a law that provides for payment to victims of violent crime to cover their economic losses. Administers state and federal grants to local crime victim assistance programs. Houses Ohio's Missing Persons Clearinghouse. Offers the Identity Theft Verification Passport Program and Victim Services Directory.

locations.name: Ohio Attorney General'S Office - Consumer Protection Section
locations.description: Enforces Ohio laws that protect consumers and businesses. Investigates complaints related to consumer protection laws and mediates disputes when warranted.

locations.name: Ohio Attorney General'S Office - Bureau Of Criminal Investigation
locations.description: Provides scientific and investigative assistance to local law enforcement agencies throughout Ohio. Operates DNA and crime laboratories and maintains the Ohio Automated Fingerprint Identification System.

locations.name: Ohio Attorney General'S Office - Health Care Fraud Section
locations.description: Investigates and prosecutes healthcare providers accused of defrauding the state's Medicaid program and enforces Ohio's Patient Abuse and Neglect Laws, which protect the mentally and physically disabled and the elderly from exploitation and abuse in Ohio's long-term care facilities. Prosecutes claimants, employers, or health care providers who defraud the Ohio Workers' Compensation Program.

locations.name: Ohio Attorney General'S Office - Charitable Law Section
locations.description: Enforces the Ohio Charitable Solicitations Act, which regulates all charitable trusts within Ohio and licenses charitable bingo games. Conducts statewide investigations, often in cooperation with outside law enforcement officials, and represents the Attorney General in all civil litigation and administrative proceedings arising under the state laws that regulate charitable organizations.

locations.name: Ohio Attorney General'S Office - Cleveland Office
locations.description: Functions as the Regional Office of the Ohio Attorney General.

schedules.description: M-F: 8:00am-5:30pm, staff not always in the office.

data.name: Ohio Means Jobs - Cleveland - Cuyahoga County
alternateNames: Cuyahoga County Department of Workforce Development
data.description: Provides job search and job readiness assistance and access to education and training to those who are unemployed or underemployed. Operates several career center sites and provides assistance through partnerships with other public and nonprofit agencies.

services.name: Employment Assistance For Displaced / Laid Off Workers
services.description: Provides career change counseling, job training, and job finding assistance.
services.applicationProcess: Phone for information or to schedule an intake appointment.

eligibility.description: Adults 18 and older who are residents of the State of Ohio and are a displaced worker or homemaker. DISPLACED WORKERS: must have been laid off from their jobs. Must apply for unemployment benefits before applying for this program, but may apply for them before actually receiving unemployment.  However, if you are denied for unemployment benefits, then you will not be accepted in this program. DISPLACED HOMEMAKERS:  Must have been providing unpaid homemaking services for family members and are no longer being supported financially by a family member.  Must also be unemployed or underemployed and having difficulty in obtaining employment.

documents.description: For orientation will need: Driver's license OR State I.D. (Ohio); Resume or current job history; Social Security Card; Proof of Citizenship and Date/Place of Birth:  Birth Certificate, Unexpired Passport, Resident Alien Card, ISS1or l151 Form, or Naturalization papers; Selective Service Enrollment Card or Verifications (for males born after December 31, 1959).   Laid off workers may also need:  letter of lay off printed on company letterhead; last three pay stubs; unemployment benefits, notification of benefit rights letter and determination of benefits letter.  Displaced homemakers may also need: proof of income for last six months for self and spouse; proof of family size; parole release papers (if applicable) and one of the following: divorce papers; separation papers; spouse's death certificate; spouse's letter of lay off and unemployment benefits; notification of benefit rights letter; determination of benefits letter; last three paycheck stubs from part-time employment; will be advised of any other needed documents.

services.name: Youth Resource Center
alternateNames: COMPREHENSIVE CASE MANAGEMENT AND EMPLOYMENT PROGRAM (CCMEP)
services.description: Provides support, guidance with personal challenges, and workshops to help build confidence in securing employment. Services include connection to education and training, work readiness, career exploration, preparation for GED/High School Equivalency tests and high school graduation tests, resume and job skills preparation, use of computers for employment, and assistance with removing barriers to employment that may exist. "Participants are provided with case management to address barriers to self-sufficiency such as health, addiction/substance abuse issues, housing, education, transportation, etc."
services.applicationProcess: Phone for appointment or leave a message that will be returned. May visit website for more information.

eligibility.description: Youth ages 14 through 24 who are residents of Cuyahoga County.

documents.description: Birth certificate, proof of residency and income, Social Security card, Parental Consent.

services.name: Employment Assistance For Adults
services.description: Provides a vocational training and job placement service for unemployed or under-employed individuals. Includes career change counseling and assistance for displaced workers and displaced homemakers. Those interested in job training will first complete career testing and assessment before being placed in an appropriate training program. Offers all participants workshops, computer and telephone access, resume development, and other job search assistance. Maintains a database for job seekers to post their resumes for potential employers to view.

schedules.description: Orientation times vary by site and are subject to change. Regular office hours: Downtown: M-F: 8:00am-4:30pm.

services.applicationProcess: Must first attend an orientation session. Phone to register for an orientation session or for more information. May also go to the website to register and to post a resume on the Job-Seeker Database. If client does not have a resume arrive early for scheduled orientation session.

eligibility.description: Adults 18 years or older who are residents of Cuyahoga County.

documents.description: For orientation will need: Driver's license OR State I.D. (Ohio); Resume or current job history; Social Security Card; Proof of Citizenship and Date/Place of Birth:  Birth Certificate, Unexpired Passport, Resident Alien Card, ISS1or l151 Form, or Naturalization papers; Selective Service Enrollment Card or Verifications (for males born after December 31, 1959).  After orientation may also need proof of residency: postmarked mail or utility bill dated in the past 30 days; DD-214 or Veteran certificate, if applicable; parole release papers, if applicable.

services.name: The Resource Room
services.description: Provides resources for job seekers. Available resources include the use of a computer lab, internet access, a job search database, phones to file for unemployment services, fax and copying services, assistance with resumes, and workshops on resume development, labor market information, and interviewing skills. Services may vary with site.

schedules.description: Downtown location: M-F: 8:00am-4:30pm. Old Brooklyn and Quincy Place are currently closed.

services.applicationProcess: Walk-in for service. May also phone for information M-F: 8:00am-4:30pm. Mask wearing optional.

fees.description: Free for use of computers, workshops; may be fee for copies, faxes etc.

eligibility.description: Adults age 18 or older in Cuyahoga County. Targeted to unemployed persons seeking employment.

services.name: Cuyahoga County Educational Assistance Program (EAP)
services.description: Provides financial assistance for Cuyahoga County residents seeking a post-secondary degree and/or program certification that will lead to gainful employment or career advancement. Offers scholarships up to 5,000 dollars to qualified applicants to complete the program.
services.applicationProcess: Visit the Cuyahogacounty.us website (Educational Assistance link) for CCEAP information, including the list of CCEAP training Providers. Visit the OMJCC.us website (Job Seekers link) to call, register, and start the application for funding process.

fees.description: Free. Funds will be applied at the beginning of the qualifying term.

eligibility.description: Cuyahoga County residents age 18 or older who is within 12 credit hours of completion of qualifying certificate or degree. Can receive up to a maximum of 18 months, including summer sessions, to complete the approved term. Must be enrolled or plan to enroll in one of the participating training program providers (includes many of the local and state universities in Ohio).

documents.description: Social security card; Photo I.D.; Proof of enrollment.

services.name: Jobs For Veterans State Grant (JVSG)
services.description: Provides assistance for veterans with employment barriers. Helps with job placement assistance as well as employment readiness training such as resume building and military experience conversion.
services.applicationProcess: Phone for information.

eligibility.description: Veterans looking for employment.

services.name: Career Fairs
services.description: Provides a virtual event where online participants can communicate with local employers

schedules.description: Held through out the year. Most events employers will be available to chat between 10:00am-2:00pm.

services.applicationProcess: Must pre-register. Register via website (omjcc.easyvirtualfair.com) or phone for more information. Will need to use Google Chrome as the web browser.

eligibility.description: Those seeking employment.

services.name: Rapid Response Hub
services.description: Provides career change counseling, job training, and job finding assistance to those effected by mass layoffs.

schedules.description: Service is activated when a company issues a mass layoff notice under the WARN act.

services.applicationProcess: Phone for more information M-F: 8:00am-4:30pm.

eligibility.description: Those who have been effected by a mass layoff.

locations.name: Ohio Means Jobs - Cleveland - Cuyahoga County - Downtown Location
alternateNames: Cuyahoga County Department of Workforce Development
locations.description: Provides job search and job readiness assistance and access to education and training to those who are unemployed or underemployed. Operates several career center sites and provides assistance through partnerships with other public and nonprofit agencies.

locations.name: Ohio Means Jobs - Cleveland - Cuyahoga County - Young Adult Resource Center (YRC)
alternateNames: Cuyahoga County Department of Workforce Development
locations.description: Provides job search and job readiness assistance and access to education and training for those who are unemployed or under-employed.

locations.name: Ohio Means Jobs - Cleveland - Cuyahoga County At Quincy Place
alternateNames: Cuyahoga County Department of Workforce Development
locations.description: Provides access to vocational training, job placement and supportive social services for unemployed or under-employed individuals.

data.name: Cleveland Metroparks
data.description: Maintains open spaces, parkways and recreational facilities including the Cleveland Metroparks Zoo and nature centers.  Offers golf courses, a marina, swimming areas and lakes, stables, a toboggan run, sledding hills, hiking trails and picnic areas.

services.name: Recreation - Outdoors
services.description: Provides a variety of recreational facilities and activities. Facilities includes public golf courses, marinas, swimming areas and lakes, stables, a toboggan run, sledding hills, hiking trails and picnic areas.  Activities include golf, swimming, boating, sledding, cross country skiing, fishing/icefishing, tobogganing and hiking. Also provides a variety of programming focused on developing necessary skills for safe activities while camping or in the outdoors. Classes are available for all skill levels. Offerings include: camp cooking, first aid, boating and canoeing skills, bicycle maintenance, fishing techniques, backpacking, and map and compass basics.

schedules.description: All Metroparks Reservations are open daily from 6:00am-11:00pm.

services.applicationProcess: Phone M-F: 8:00am-5:00pm or email or check website for information.

fees.description: Free and/or fixed fee, depending upon activity offered.

eligibility.description: No restrictions.

services.name: Speakers Bureau
services.description: Provides expert speakers who are available to speak on a wide variety of topics concerning animals, nature and the environment for community groups.  Also offers programs for those in nursing homes and hospitals.

schedules.description: As scheduled between the sponsoring group and the Metroparks.

services.applicationProcess: Phone M-F: 8:30am-4:30pm for information or to schedule a presentation.

eligibility.description: Generally, serves groups within 60 miles of main site but may make exceptions.

services.name: Zoo
services.description: Operates a zoo and rainforest that includes indoor and outdoor exhibits of wild and indigenous animals and birds. Also has an aquatic life exhibit.

schedules.description: The Zoo is open daily: 10:00am-5:00pm from April through October and daily: 10:00am-4:00pm from November through March. The Box Office closes one hour prior to the Zoo's closure. The Zoo is closed on Thanksgiving Day, Christmas Day, and New Year's Day. The Zoo is open additional hours for special events.

services.applicationProcess: Walk in during Zoo hours or phone for information M-F: 9:00am-5:00pm or check website for information. The Box Office closes 1 hour prior to the Zoo's closure.

fees.description: Zoo admission includes The Zoo and The Rain Forest. Fixed fee except for Mondays which are free (Zoo only) to residents of Cuyahoga County and Hinckley Township. From Labor Day through Memorial Day, when the Monday is a holiday, the free day is on Tuesday, the day after the holiday.  Children under age 2 are free. There are special seasonal rates in the winter, as well as Polar Bear Days (below 32 degrees) with even more discounted fees. Rental of children's strollers and wheelchairs for adults is extra. Parking is free.

eligibility.description: No restrictions.

documents.description: Fee for admission. Varied rates for non-members - general admission ticket price categories are for ages 2-11, ages 12-61, and ages 62 and older. Tickets are not required for children under age 2. Discounted tickets available for military personnel, veterans, and their family members. Some individual exhibits have a fixed fee.

services.name: Space Rental
services.description: Provides day and evening space rental for meetings and events for community groups and the public. Also rents picnic areas and shelter rentals.
services.applicationProcess: Fill out the Event Request Form on the website in order to be contacted by the Cleveland Metroparks event team. Check website for information, including availability of sites.

eligibility.description: No restrictions.

services.name: Recreational / Educational Programming - Zoo
services.description: Provides a wide range of recreational and educational events and programming for all ages, with a focus of ecology, natural preservation, biology, wildlife, and nature.
services.applicationProcess: Information and registration is available through the website. Phone M-F: 9:00am-5:00pm or email for information.

eligibility.description: Varies by program and activity.

services.name: Community Education - Nature
services.description: Provides a wide variety of programs about nature, geography, history and the environment. Offerings may include lectures, hikes, crafts, games, hands-on experiences, bird walks, photography and films, and other similar activities.  Each Nature Center is staffed with naturalists that can answer questions about nature, wildlife, and the environment.  Offers programs specific to youth and those that are home schooled. Offers mobile classrooms to provide educational content (for organizations, groups, and special events).

schedules.description: Varies by activity and site; call for dates and times.

services.applicationProcess: Phone or check website for information and for program schedules.  To ask a naturalist a question, phone or walk into a Nature Center daily 9:30am-5:00pm.

fees.description: Some programs are free; Some are fixed fee.

eligibility.description: Some activities may be for specific ages (both yoth and adults). Special classes and activities may be requested by organizations and groups.

services.name: Day Camps
services.description: Offers a variety of summer day camps for a wide range of ages. Activities vary from program to program but generally include outdoor activities such as hiking, kayaking, crafts, and natural sciences.

schedules.description: Varies as scheduled, but generally held during the summer. Duration may be from 1-7 days.

services.applicationProcess: Most registration for day camps is by website only. May phone M-F: 8:00am-5:00pm for information about the camps or for information about possible scholarships. Registration begins in early March and camps fill up quickly.

fees.description: Fixed fee; scholarships may be available.

eligibility.description: Age range will vary depending on the program. Generally, summer camps are open to ages 3 through 18.

documents.description: Completed registration form, Administer Medications form, and Release/waiver.

locations.name: Cleveland Metroparks
locations.description: Maintains open spaces, parkways and recreational facilities including the Cleveland Metroparks Zoo and nature centers.  Offers golf courses, a marina, swimming areas and lakes, stables, a toboggan run, sledding hills, hiking trails and picnic areas.

locations.name: Cleveland Metroparks - Zoo And The Rainforest
locations.description: Operates the Cleveland Metroparks Zoo and Rainforest.  Includes both indoor and outdoor exhibits of wild and indigenous animals and birds and an aquatic life exhibit.

phones.name: Voice
phones.description: Cleveland Metroparks Zoo - general information 24 hours/day

phones.name: Voice
phones.description: Cleveland Metroparks Zoo - general information 24 hours/day

phones.name: Voice
phones.description: Cleveland Metroparks Zoo - general information 24 hours/day

phones.name: Voice
phones.description: Cleveland Metroparks Zoo - general information 24 hours/day

data.name: Post-Polio Health International
data.description: Provides advocacy for, and supports research and education about, issues relating to Post-Polio Syndrome (PPS). Also provides information about a network of PPS support groups.

services.name: Health Information
services.description: Provides information regarding current research, treatment options and lifestyle adaptation strategies for those affected by Post-Polio Syndrome (PPS). Publishes a quarterly newsletter, and makes additional information available online. Also offers various levels of membership; members receive additional updates, newsletters and publications through the agencies partnership with other polio-related organizations.
services.applicationProcess: Phone or visit website for information or to inquire about membership.

fees.description: Fixed fee for membership and print copies of newsletter (digital copies are archived online, and access is free).

eligibility.description: No restrictions.

services.name: Health Support Groups - Post-Polio Syndrome
services.description: Offers one-on-one support for those living with Post-Polio Syndrome (PPS). In-person support groups may be available in some areas of the country.

schedules.description: Office hours: M-F: 9:30am-5:30pm EST. Hours may vary for support as set between client and agency.

services.applicationProcess: Call for one-on-one support or information. Can also visit website for information (note: do not list support group sites on website.)

eligibility.description: No restrictions.

locations.name: Post-Polio Health International
locations.description: Provides advocacy for, and supports research and education about, issues relating to Post-Polio Syndrome (PPS). Also provides information about a network of PPS support groups.

data.name: Senior Transportation Connection
data.description: Provides transportation to seniors and adults with disabilities in a number of municipalities in Cuyahoga County.

services.name: Door To Door Transportation
services.description: Provides transportation for any purpose (medical appointments, shopping, hairdresser, etc.), rides to the polls during election season for seniors living in partner communities. In general, destinations are limited to the suburbs served, with clients remaining on their own side of the county. There are a few exceptions, such as some suburban medical facilities outside the immediate service area, but in most cases all trips outside partner communities would be only on a space- and time-available basis.

schedules.description: Call Center: M-F: 7:00am-5:00pm; Vehicle Hours: M-F: 8:30am-4:30pm. Holidays are recognized in accordance with client's municipality. School closings due to snow will NOT affect this service - if service has to be cancelled, scheduled clients will be notified directly.

services.applicationProcess: Phone to schedule by 12:00noon, 3 business days prior to the needed trip. New clients may schedule trips up to 7 days in advance. Those with regular appointments at the same time(s) on the same day(s) may schedule up to 14 days in advance. A message can be left on answering machine and call will be returned. On first call, information will be obtained, including contact information, relevant health information, etc. First-time clients from City of Cleveland must first call intake number at the Dept. of Aging to register for service, M-F: 8:00am-5:00pm. Registration form can be mailed out or picked up from local senior centers. In some cases, registration can be completed over the phone. NOTE: Some municipal and nonprofit providers expand or limit eligibility by age (i.e. seniors 55 and older) or specialized populations (i.e. persons with disabilities).

fees.description: Small fixed fees each way, higher if leaving the municipality service area (subject to availability).

eligibility.description:
Open to those age 60 or older, with or without disabilities, who live in partner communities. The following cities allow their residents to register directly with STC: Middleburg Heights, North Olmsted, Richmond Heights and University Heights.

Some municipal and nonprofit providers expand or limit eligibility by age (i.e. seniors 55 and older) or specialized populations (i.e. persons with disabilities).

services.name: Special Group Trips
services.description: Offers transportation for a group of people traveling from one destination to another destination and back..
services.applicationProcess: Phone for more information M-F: 7:00am-5:00pm.

eligibility.description: Organizations in Cuyahoga county affiliated with STC.

locations.name: Senior Transportation Connection
locations.description: Provides transportation for seniors in a number of municipalities in Cuyahoga County.

data.name: Auglaize County Department Of Job And Family Services
data.description: Offers telephone information about where to access health and human service needs in Auglaize County. Provides information about a range of services including food, shelter, health care, substance abuse, mental health, social activities and reporting abuse and neglect.

services.name: Comprehensive Information And Referral
services.description: Offers telephone information about where to access health and human service needs in Auglaize County. Provides information about a range of services including food, shelter, health care, substance abuse, mental health, social activities and reporting abuse and neglect.

schedules.description: M-F: 8:00am-4:30pm and possibly other hours by appointment.

services.applicationProcess: Phone for information during hours of service.

eligibility.description: Serves Auglaize County residents, but people may phone from other areas on their behalf.

locations.name: Auglaize County Department Of Job And Family Services
alternateNames: Auglaize County Dept. of Job and Family Services
locations.description: Offers telephone information about where to access health and human service needs in Auglaize County. Provides information about a range of services including food, shelter, health care, substance abuse, mental health, social activities and reporting abuse and neglect.

data.name: Cleveland Museum Of Art - The
data.description: Provides a comprehensive visual arts museum for the community. The permanent collection includes 61,000 objects and spans 6,000 years of achievement in the arts from around the world. Considered one of the top comprehensive art museums in the nation. Offers special exhibits and educational and cultural opportunities for the public.

services.name: Museum - Visual Arts
services.description: Provides a comprehensive visual arts museum for the community. The permanent collection of over 61,000 works of art covers a span of 6,000 years from ancient Egypt to the present, and includes masterpieces from Europe, Asia, African and the Americas. Offers a significant forum for exhibitions, scholarship, and performing arts.
services.applicationProcess: Walk in for service.

fees.description: Tickets are free; may be a charge for special exhibits or programs. There is an annual membership option available; members pay less or nothing for some events/programs. There is a charge for parking.

eligibility.description: To maintain social distancing guidelines, a limited number of visitors are permitted in the building per day. Tickets are limited to eight per person.

services.name: Ingalls Library And Museum Archives
services.description: Provides an art museum library that includes a book collection, a comprehensive image library and photographic and museum archives. Materials are not available for loan.
services.applicationProcess: Phone for information M-F: 9:00am-5:00pm. When planning a visit, plan ahead and submit hold requests for the needed resources before visiting. Materials are retrieved from the library stacks at 9:30am,11:00am,12:30pm,2:00pm, and 3:30pm.

eligibility.description: Age 18 and older.

documents.description: A library account is required to place holds.

services.name: Summer Creativity Camps
services.description: Provides age-appropriate experiences to appreciate and participate in creating art.

schedules.description: Camps are one week long from June through July. Call for more information on scheduling.

services.applicationProcess: Registration is online.

eligibility.description: Ages 5-16 years old.

locations.name: Cleveland Museum Of Art - The
locations.description: Provides a comprehensive visual arts museum for the community. The permanent collection includes 61,000 objects and spans 6,000 years of achievement in the arts from around the world. Considered one of the top comprehensive art museums in the nation. Offers special exhibits and educational and cultural opportunities for the public.

schedules.description: Main Museum hours - Tu, Th, Sa, Su: 10:00am-5:00pm. W, F: 10:00am-9:00pm. Closed on Mondays.

locations.name: Cleveland Museum Of Art - The - Community Arts Center
locations.description: Cleveland Museum of Art's Community Arts Center that offers free art classes, youth and family programs and workshops, art making and community events.

data.name: United Way Of North Central Ohio
data.description: Offers telephone information about where to access health and human service needs in Seneca County. Provides information about a range of services including food, shelter, health care, substance abuse, mental health, recreation and social activities.

services.name: First Call For Help
services.description: Offers telephone information about where to access health and human service needs in Seneca County. Provides information about a range of services including food, shelter, health care, substance abuse, mental health, recreation and social activities.
services.applicationProcess: Phone for information M-F: 8:00am-5:00pm.

eligibility.description: Serves Seneca County residents, but people from other areas may phone on their behalf.

locations.name: United Way Of North Central Ohio
locations.description: Offers telephone information about where to access health and human service needs in Seneca County. Provides information about a range of services including food, shelter, health care, substance abuse, mental health, recreation and social activities.

data.name: Gmn Tri-County Community Action Committee
data.description: Offers telephone information about where to access health and human service needs in Guernsey, Monroe, and Noble Counties.

services.name: Comprehensive Information And Referral
services.description: Offers information about where to access health and human service needs in Guernsey, Monroe, and Noble Counties. Provides information about a range of services, including many that offer assistance with food, shelter, health care, substance abuse treatment, mental health, recreation and social activities.
services.applicationProcess: Phone for information M-F: 8:00am-4:30pm (may also contact through form on website anytime).

eligibility.description: Serves residents of Guernsey, Monroe, and Noble Counties; but people from other areas may phone on their behalf.

locations.name: Gmn Tri-County Community Action Committee
locations.description: Offers telephone information about where to access health and human service needs in Guernsey, Monroe, and Noble Counties.

data.name: Cleveland Museum Of Natural History
data.description: Provides a comprehensive natural history museum for the community, offering both permanent and temporary exhibits. Also has a planetarium, observatory, and outdoor gardens. Offers educational programs for the public.

services.name: Museum - Natural History
services.description: Provides a comprehensive natural history museum for the community. Permanent exhibits range from dinosaurs and exotic cultures, to rare diamonds and distant planets.  Also offers a Discovery Center, traveling exhibits, live animal shows, outdoor gardens, a Museum store and café. Offers tours on request.
services.applicationProcess: Phone for information or walk-in for service Tu-Su: 10:00am-5:00pm.

fees.description: Fixed fee; discounts available for members, seniors, and students with ID. Free for children under 2 years of age. Group rates are available. Paid parking is required.

eligibility.description: No restrictions.

services.name: The Nathan And Fannye Shafran Planetarium
services.description: Provides a planetarium and interactive exhibits about the cosmos. Also serves as the home site of the Cleveland Astronomical Society.
services.applicationProcess: Phone for information or walk in for service Tu-Su: 10:00am-5:00pm.

fees.description: Fixed fee in addition to museum admission; discounts available for seniors, children under 6 years of age, and college students with ID. Free for members. Group rates may be available.

eligibility.description: Most programs target those age 6 and older. Periodically schedules special programming for younger children.

documents.description: Proof of paid admission if a non-member.

services.name: The Harold Terry Clark Library
services.description: Provides a specialized library of over 60,000 volumes focusing on the natural sciences. The library currently receives 650 different titles of periodicals through subscriptions, gifts and inter-institutional exchanges. Also has a large collection of maps, reprint and pamphlet files, slides and audio materials, and some extremely rare book collections of Audubon, Gould, Curtis and North-American Indians. In addition, houses archival records about the Museum, professional papers, and special collections about people/events associated with the Museum.
services.applicationProcess: Public access by appointment only. Phone for information or appointment M-F: 9:00am-5:00pm .

fees.description: Free. There is a charge for parking.

eligibility.description: No restrictions.

services.name: Community Education - Natural History / Astronomy
services.description: Provides a variety of classes, workshops, field trips and travel opportunities focusing on the natural sciences and astronomy. Offers some programs specifically for youth and families.
services.applicationProcess: Phone for information or to register M-F: 9:00am-5:00pm. Information also available on website.

fees.description: Fixed fee for some activities, free for others; Discounts available for members, seniors, and students with ID. Group rates may be available. There is a charge for parking.

eligibility.description: Adults, age 18 and older. There are no geographic restrictions.

services.name: Day Camps - Natural Sciences
services.description: Offers day camps for youth that focus on science and the natural world.
services.applicationProcess: Information or and registration is available on the website. May also email for more information. Registration begins in early Winter of each year.

fees.description: Fixed fee; less if member of participating organizations. Limited number of scholarships are available.

eligibility.description: Serves youth in grade 1 through 12 (generally ages 5 through 18).

locations.name: Cleveland Museum Of Natural History
locations.description: Provides a comprehensive natural history museum for the community, offering both permanent and temporary exhibits. Also has a planetarium, observatory, and outdoor gardens. Offers educational programs for the public.
